event.headline: Peloton announces $420 million acquisition of Precor to enhance U.S. manufacturing and R&D capabilities.
event.detail: On December 21, 2020, Peloton entered into a Stock and Asset Purchase Agreement to acquire Precor for $420 million, aiming to establish U.S. manufacturing capacity and enhance R&D with nearly 100 new employees.
doc_summaries: Peloton announces agreement to acquire Precor for $420M to establish U.S. manufacturing capacity
Press release detailing the Precor acquisition — U.S. manufacturing, R&D, and commercial-market expansion plans

snippets:
# Press Release: Peloton Signs Agreement to Acquire Precor
**New York, NY — December 21, 2020**

Acquisition expected to establish Peloton's U.S. manufacturing footprint, enhance R&D
capabilities, and accelerate growth of commercial verticals.

Peloton (NASDAQ: PTON), the leading interactive fitness platform, today announced that it has
entered into an agreement to acquire Precor, one of the largest global commercial fitness
equipment providers with a significant U.S. manufacturing presence, in a transaction valued at
$420 million USD. With the acquisition, Peloton plans to establish U.S. manufacturing capacity,
boost research and development capabilities with Precor's highly-skilled team, and accelerate
Peloton's penetration of the commercial market. Peloton plans to produce connected fitness
products in the U.S. before the end of calendar year 2021.

Precor President Rob Barker will become CEO, Precor and General Manager, Peloton Commercial,
reporting to William Lynch, Peloton's President. Precor is a division of Finnish sporting
goods company Amer Sports. The transaction is expected to close in early calendar year 2021.

**"Precor embodies the Peloton mission of putting Members first,"** said Peloton's Lynch. "By
combining our talented and committed R&D and Supply Chain teams with the incredibly capable
Precor team and their decades of experience, we believe we will be able to lead the global
connected fitness market in both innovation and scale."

## Establishing U.S. manufacturing
The acquisition adds 625,000 square feet of U.S. manufacturing capacity in Whitsett, North
Carolina and Woodinville, Washington. Peloton will be able to control the entire production
process, from design to ship, and increase total production scale.

## Investing more in research and development
The acquisition adds a team of nearly 100 dedicated R&D employees to Peloton's team.

## Scaling Peloton commercial offerings
Precor has 40 years of experience building a customer-focused commercial fitness business at
scale, with long-standing relationships with hotels, multifamily residences, and college and
corporate campuses.

**About Peloton:** Peloton is the leading interactive fitness platform in the world with a
loyal community of more than 3.6 million Members. Founded in 2012, headquartered in New York
City.

# Peloton Interactive, Inc. — Form 8-K
**Date of Report:** December 21, 2020

## Item 1.01 — Entry into a Material Definitive Agreement

**Stock and Asset Purchase Agreement**

On December 21, 2020, Peloton Interactive, Inc., a Delaware corporation ("Peloton"), entered
into a Stock and Asset Purchase Agreement ("Purchase Agreement") with Amer Sports Corporation,
a Finnish corporation ("Parent"), pursuant to which Peloton will acquire Precor Incorporated,
a Delaware corporation ("Precor"), and certain related entities and assets from Parent and
certain of its subsidiaries (the "Acquisition"). Precor manufactures, sells and distributes
cardiovascular and strength-building exercise machines, fitness equipment and associated
technical and digital services.

Under the terms of the Purchase Agreement, Peloton has agreed to acquire Precor for $420.0
million in cash, subject to customary adjustments for working capital, transaction expenses,
cash and indebtedness. The closing of the transactions is anticipated to occur in early
calendar year 2021 and is subject to customary closing conditions, including expiration or
termination of the applicable waiting period under the Hart-Scott-Rodino Antitrust
Improvements Act of 1976.

**Press Release**

On December 21, 2020, Peloton issued a press release announcing the execution of the Purchase
Agreement. This press release is furnished as Exhibit 99.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K.

## Item 9.01 — Financial Statements and Exhibits

(d) Exhibits: Exhibit 99.1 — Press release dated December 21, 2020.

**Signature:** Hisao Kushi, Chief Legal Officer

On December 21, 2020, Peloton Interactive, Inc. issued a press release announcing the execution of the Purchase Agreement, which is furnished as Exhibit 99.1 to the Form 8-K report. (Date/Time: 2020-12-21)

Peloton entered into an agreement to acquire Precor, one of the largest global commercial fitness equipment providers, in a transaction valued at $420 million USD. The acquisition is intended to establish a U.S. manufacturing footprint, enhance R&D capabilities, and accelerate growth in commercial verticals. (Date/Time: December 21, 2020)
Rob Barker, in his role as CEO of Precor and General Manager of Peloton Commercial, reports to William Lynch, the President of Peloton.

# Peloton Interactive, Inc. — Form 8-K

## Item 2.02 — Results of Operations and Financial Condition

On August 26, 2021, Peloton Interactive, Inc. ("Peloton" or the "Company") will hold a
conference call regarding its financial results for the year ended June 30, 2021. Peloton also
issued a letter to its stockholders announcing its financial results for the year ended June
30, 2021 (the "Shareholder Letter"). A copy of the Shareholder Letter is furnished as Exhibit

Peloton is making reference to non-GAAP financial information in both the Shareholder Letter
and the conference call. A reconciliation of GAAP to non-GAAP results is provided in the

shipping to reduce order-to-delivery windows, which we were pleased to restore to
pre-pandemic levels during the fourth quarter.

To support our large long-term growth opportunity, on May 24th we announced plans to build our
first U.S. factory. **Peloton Output Park (POP)** will sit on over 200 acres of land in Troy
Township, Ohio and will feature more than a million square feet of manufacturing, office, and
amenities space, making it one of the largest connected fitness plants in the world. This
factory will diversify our manufacturing footprint, provide additional capacity to support our
growth, drive production efficiencies through automation and scale, and accelerate our
speed-to-market. POP will complement our existing manufacturing assets in Taiwan and the
recently acquired Precor assets in North Carolina and Washington state. We expect to begin
producing Bike, Bike+, and Tread products at POP starting in calendar 2023.

We also announced our latest step in making Peloton more attainable by lowering the price of
our original Peloton Bike across all markets to $1,495 USD. We officially launched the Peloton
Corporate Wellness program, with initial launch partners including Wayfair, Samsung, SAP,
Accenture Interactive, and Sky, and our first partnership with a health insurer, United
Healthcare. We're pleased to report that on August 30th we will officially launch sales of the
Peloton Tread in the U.S. while resuming sales in the U.K. and Canada.

We ended the quarter with 2.33 million Connected Fitness Subscriptions, representing 114%
year-over-year growth. Total platform workouts grew to over 154 million in the quarter from 83
million in the year-ago quarter.

### Internal Controls Update
As you will see in our upcoming Annual Report on Form 10-K, in the course of our fiscal 2021
audit process, a material weakness was identified in our internal controls over financial
reporting with respect to identification and valuation of inventory. This was a result of
discovering an immaterial discrepancy in our year-end physical inventory counts. It did not
result in a material misstatement of our financial statements. We are committed to fully
remediating these issues as soon as possible.

### Q1 and Full Fiscal Year 2022 Business Outlook

**FY2022 Q1 Guidance:**
- 2.47 million ending Connected Fitness Subscriptions
- Average Net Monthly Connected Fitness Churn of approximately 0.85%

# Press Release: Peloton Announces Appointment of New Directors to the Board
**New York — February 8, 2022**

Angel L. Mendez and Jonathan Mildenhall to Join the Peloton Board; Longtime Director Erik
Blachford to Step Down. **Barry McCarthy, Newly Named CEO and President of Peloton, to Also
Join Board; John Foley to Become Executive Chair.**

Peloton Interactive, Inc. (NASDAQ: PTON) today announced the appointment of two new directors
to its Board: Angel L. Mendez, a supply chain leader, and Jonathan Mildenhall, a creative
leader in marketing and advertising. Erik Blachford, a director since 2015, will step down.

In addition, **Barry McCarthy, Peloton's newly named CEO and President, will join the Board as
a director, and John Foley has been named Executive Chair.**

With these changes, Peloton's Board will comprise nine directors, four of whom will have been
appointed in the past three years.

**John Foley, Co-Founder of Peloton and Executive Chair, said:** "We are pleased to welcome
Barry, Angel and Jonathan as new directors to the Peloton Board at this important time. We are
confident they will provide valuable perspectives as we continue to execute our strategy,
drive profitability, and enhance value for all Peloton shareholders."

**Pamela Thomas-Graham, Chair of the Nominating, Governance and Corporate Responsibility
Committee, said:** "These appointments are the culmination of a robust search process that
began several months ago."

Foley concluded: "On behalf of the entire Board, I thank Erik for his valuable perspective and
incredible commitment to Peloton over the past nearly seven years."

## About Angel L. Mendez
Executive Chairman of the Board of LevaData Inc. Previously EVP and COO of HERE Technologies
(2016-2020), and senior executive at Cisco Systems (2005-2015).

## About Jonathan Mildenhall
Co-founder and Chair of TwentyFirstCenturyBrand. Previously CMO of Airbnb (2014-2018), during
which Airbnb's valuation increased from $1 billion to more than $31 billion. Previously led
Coca-Cola's marketing initiatives.

Additional information regarding McCarthy's appointment as Peloton's new CEO and President can
be found in a separate press release issued today.

# Peloton Interactive, Inc. — Form 8-K
**Date of Report:** February 5, 2022

## Item 5.02 — Departure of Directors or Certain Officers; Election of Directors; Appointment of Certain Officers

### Director Changes
On February 5, 2022, Erik Blachford notified the Board of his resignation as a Class I
director and from the Compensation and NCGR Committees, effective February 8, 2022. Mr.
Blachford's resignation is not the result of any disagreement with the Company.

On February 7, 2022, the Board increased its size from seven to nine directors and appointed
Angel L. Mendez and Jonathan Mildenhall to the Board.

### CEO Transition; Executive Chair
On February 7, 2022, the Board appointed **Barry McCarthy as Chief Executive Officer and
President**, succeeding **John Foley**, the Company's Co-Founder and former CEO, effective
February 9, 2022. Mr. Foley was appointed **Executive Chair** of the Board. Mr. McCarthy was
also appointed to the Board.

Mr. McCarthy, 68, most recently served as Spotify's Chief Financial Officer from 2015 to
January 2020. From 1999 to 2010, he served as CFO and Principal Accounting Officer of Netflix,
Inc. He has served on the boards of Spotify and Instacart, and previously served on the boards
of Chegg, Eventbrite, MSD Acquisition Corp, Pandora, and Rent the Runway.

Under his Offer Letter, Mr. McCarthy is eligible for: (i) an annual base salary of $1,000,000,
(ii) a stock option award to purchase 8,000,000 shares of Class A Common Stock, (iii) up to
$150,000 relocation reimbursement, and (iv) up to $30,000 legal cost reimbursement.

### President Transition
In connection with Mr. McCarthy's appointment as President, **William Lynch** will transition
from this position to a non-executive director on the Board.

## Item 9.01 — Financial Statements and Exhibits

(d) Exhibits:
- 10.1 — Offer Letter between Barry McCarthy and the Registrant, dated February 7, 2022
- 99.1 — Press Release Announcing Board Appointments, dated February 8, 2022
- 99.2 — Press Release Announcing Leadership Transitions, dated February 8, 2022

**Signature:** Hisao Kushi, Chief Legal and Culture Officer

# Press Release: Peloton Announces Comprehensive Program to Reduce Costs and Drive Growth, Profitability, and Free Cash Flow
**New York — February 8, 2022**

Realigning Business Operations, Reducing Manufacturing Footprint, and Enhancing Capital and
Operational Efficiency. **Expects to Deliver at Least $800 Million in Annual Run-Rate Cost
Savings; Reduces Planned 2022 CapEx Spending by Approximately $150 Million.**

Peloton Interactive, Inc. (NASDAQ: PTON) today announced a series of steps it is taking to
position the business for long-term growth while establishing a clear path to consistent
profitability and sustainable free cash flow. Once these actions are fully implemented, the
company expects to achieve **at least $800 million of annual run-rate cost savings** through
operating expense efficiencies and significant margin improvement in its Connected Fitness
category. The company will also reduce its planned capital expenditures in 2022 by
approximately $150 million. The restructuring program is expected to result in approximately
$130 million in cash charges related to severance and other exit/restructuring activities, and
$80 million in non-cash charges.

**John Foley, Co-Founder of Peloton and newly appointed Executive Chair, said:** "Peloton is at
an important juncture, and we are taking decisive steps. Our focus is on building on the
already amazing Peloton Member experience, while optimizing our organization to deliver
profitable growth. This restructuring program is the result of diligent planning to address
key areas of the business and realign our operations."

As a result of the initiatives announced today, the company expects to:

- **Improve the economics of its hardware business.** The company is winding down the
  development of its Peloton Output Park (POP) manufacturing plan, resulting in $60 million in
  restructuring capital expenditures. Peloton will also optimize its logistics network by
  reducing its owned and operated warehousing and delivery footprint.
- **Right-size the organization by enacting a workforce reduction.** The workforce reduction
  will occur across nearly all business operations, resulting in the reduction of
  **approximately 2,800 global positions**. Corporate positions will be reduced by
  approximately 20%.

Peloton's roster of instructors and breadth and depth of its content will not be impacted by
these initiatives.

Foley added: "These decisions, particularly those related to our impacted Peloton team
members, were not taken lightly. We greatly value the contributions of our talented
colleagues and are committed to supporting impacted team members in their transitions."

The company also rescheduled its second quarter 2022 earnings conference call, previously

# Peloton Interactive, Inc. — Form 8-K
**Date of Report:** February 8, 2022

## Item 2.02 — Results of Operations and Financial Condition

financial results for the quarter ended December 31, 2021. Peloton has issued a letter to its
stockholders announcing its financial results for the quarterly period ended December 31, 2021
(the "Shareholder Letter"), furnished as Exhibit 99.1.

## Item 7.01 — Regulation FD Disclosure

On February 8, 2022, the Company issued a press release announcing **a restructuring plan**.
The full text of the press release is furnished as Exhibit 99.2.

## Item 9.01 — Financial Statements and Exhibits

(d) Exhibits:
- 99.1 — Letter to Shareholders, dated February 8, 2022
- 99.2 — Press Release Announcing Restructuring Plan, dated February 8, 2022

**Signature:** Jill Woodworth, Chief Financial Officer

streamline our teams and reporting structures, increase P&L accountability, create a more
efficient and flexible supply chain, and enforce greater investment discipline.

As part of this program, **we are announcing a meaningful reduction of our workforce**, which
will impact nearly all of our operations and across almost all levels. It is not easy to say
goodbye to valued colleagues and friends, each of whom have made important contributions to
helping build Peloton into the company it is today.

When fully implemented, we expect our restructuring initiatives to yield **at least $800
million in annual run-rate cost savings** across operating expense efficiencies and material
improvements in our Connected Fitness gross margin. We have also decided to **wind down the
development of Peloton Output Park**. While we still see strategic merit in diversifying our
manufacturing footprint over the long term, we believe Tonic and our third party manufacturing
partners can support our growth for the next few years.

This has been a humbling time for Peloton, but we remain confident in the fundamentals of our
business, the strength of our platform, and the significant growth potential for Connected
Fitness and our leadership position within it. Our operational restructuring will not
negatively impact our instructor roster, number of classes produced, or range of class
modalities.

While not directly related to our restructuring, in addition to Barry joining the Board, we
also announced the appointment of two new Board members, Angel Mendez and Jonathan Mildenhall,
and thanked Erik Blachford, who is stepping down from his role on the Board.

Indeed, when I co-founded Peloton we were a five-person fitness group who aimed to bring the
community and excitement of boutique fitness into the home. Today, we are over 6.6 million
Members strong. As we look to execute on the next phase of our company's transformation, we
are clear-eyed that it will not occur overnight and that our progress may not be linear.

John

We are excited to officially announce the launch of Peloton Guide, our first connected

event.headline: Peloton appoints new CFO amid leadership transition as Jill Woodworth steps down.
event.detail: On June 6, 2022, Peloton announced the appointment of Liz Coddington as Chief Financial Officer, effective June 13, 2022, succeeding Jill Woodworth, who will serve as a consultant through September 13, 2022, to support the company in reporting its FY2022 financial results.
doc_summaries: 8-K detailing CFO transition, Woodworth to Coddington
Press release announcing Liz Coddington as CFO, succeeding Jill Woodworth

snippets:
# Press Release: Peloton Appoints Liz Coddington as Chief Financial Officer
**New York — June 6, 2022**

Peloton Interactive, Inc. (NASDAQ: PTON) today announced it has appointed **Liz Coddington**
as the company's Chief Financial Officer, effective June 13, 2022. Ms. Coddington brings more
than 20 years of experience in financial planning and analysis and operational finance. **She
succeeds Jill Woodworth, who has decided to step down as Peloton's Chief Financial Officer
after serving the company since 2018.**

Ms. Woodworth will serve as a consultant for Peloton on an interim basis, ensuring a smooth
transition and supporting the company in preparation to report its FY2022 financial results.

Most recently, Ms. Coddington served as Vice President of Finance for Amazon Web Services.
Prior to AWS, she held senior leadership roles including CFO of Adara and Walmart.com, as well
as Vice President, Financial Planning and Analysis of Netflix.

**Peloton CEO Barry McCarthy said:** "Liz is a deeply talented finance executive and will be
an invaluable addition to Peloton's leadership team. Having worked at some of the strongest and
most recognizable technology brands, she not only brings the expertise needed to run our
finance organization, but she has a critical understanding of what it takes to drive growth
and operational excellence."

Mr. McCarthy continued: "I would like to thank Jill for four years of dedicated service. She
scaled the global finance function through an extraordinary period, led the company through
its IPO, and has been an integral member of the leadership team. We wish Jill well in the next
phase of her career."

**About Peloton:** Peloton is the leading interactive fitness platform in the world with a
loyal community of more than 7 million Members. Founded in 2012, headquartered in New York
City.

# Peloton Interactive, Inc. — Form 8-K
**Date of Report:** June 6, 2022

## Item 5.02 — Departure of Directors or Certain Officers; Election of Directors; Appointment of Certain Officers

### CFO Transition
On June 6, 2022, the Board of Directors appointed **Elizabeth Coddington as Chief Financial
Officer**, to succeed **Jill Woodworth**, who is stepping down from that role, effective June
13, 2022.

Ms. Coddington, 45, brings more than 20 years of experience in financial planning and
operational finance. She most recently served as Vice President of Finance for Amazon Web
Services (January 2021 to June 2022). Prior to Amazon, she served as CFO of Adara, Inc., VP
Finance and CFO at Walmart.com, and VP Financial Planning and Analysis at Netflix, Inc.

Under her Offer Letter, Ms. Coddington is eligible for: (i) an annual base salary of
$1,000,000, (ii) an equity award valued at $9,000,000 on the date of grant, (iii) up to
$150,000 relocation reimbursement.

### Transition Agreement
In connection with Ms. Woodworth's employment separation, the Company entered into a
transition and consulting agreement with Ms. Woodworth on June 6, 2022. She will provide
consulting services through September 13, 2022, supporting the company in preparation to
report its fiscal year 2022 financial results. **Ms. Woodworth's departure is not the result of
any disagreement with the Company on any subject, including its operations, policies or
practices.**

## Item 7.01 — Regulation FD Disclosure
On June 6, 2022, the Company issued a press release announcing Ms. Coddington's appointment
and Ms. Woodworth's departure, filed as Exhibit 99.1.

## Item 9.01 — Financial Statements and Exhibits

- 10.2 — Transition and Consulting Agreement with Jill Woodworth, dated June 6, 2022

**Signature:** Hisao Kushi, Chief Legal Officer and Secretary

event.headline: Peloton announces global refinancing effort to address existing debt obligations.
event.detail: On May 20, 2024, Peloton Interactive, Inc. announced its intention to launch a global refinancing effort, proposing to offer $275.0 million aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2029 and enter into a $1.0 billion five-year term loan facility and a $100.0 million five-year revolving credit facility, intending to use the proceeds to repurchase approximately $800.0 million of its existing convertible senior notes due 2026 and refinance its existing credit facilities.
doc_summaries: 8-K announcing global refinancing: $275M convertible notes, $1.0B term loan, $100M revolver, repurchasing ~$800M existing notes
Press release detailing the refinancing terms

snippets:
# Press Release: Peloton Interactive, Inc. Announces Launch of Global Refinancing
**New York — May 20, 2024**

Peloton Interactive, Inc. ("Peloton") (Nasdaq: PTON) today announced that it intends to launch
a global refinancing, pursuant to which it will offer **$275.0 million aggregate principal
amount of convertible senior notes due 2029** (the "notes") in a private offering and enter
into a **$1.0 billion five-year term loan facility** and a **$100.0 million five-year revolving
credit facility** (together with the new term loan facility, the "new credit facilities").
Peloton also expects to grant the initial purchasers of the notes a 13-day option to purchase
up to an additional $41.3 million aggregate principal amount of the notes.

Peloton intends to use the net proceeds of the offering and the new credit facilities,
together with cash on hand, to **repurchase approximately $800.0 million of its 0.00%
convertible senior notes due 2026** (the "Existing Notes"), to refinance its existing term
loan and revolving credit facilities, and to pay fees and expenses related thereto. The
closing of the offering, the entry into the new credit facilities, and the repurchase of the
Existing Notes are not cross-conditioned upon each other, except that entry into the new
credit facilities is conditioned upon repurchasing at least $800.0 million aggregate principal
amount of the Existing Notes.

The notes will be senior, unsecured obligations of Peloton, convertible into cash, shares of
Peloton's Class A common stock, or a combination thereof, at Peloton's election. The notes
will only be offered and sold to persons reasonably believed to be qualified institutional
buyers pursuant to Rule 144A under the Securities Act of 1933.

This announcement is neither an offer to sell nor a solicitation of an offer to buy any of the
notes or any shares of Class A common stock potentially issuable upon conversion of the notes.

**Investor Relations Contact:** James Marsh
**Press Contact:** Letena Lindsay

# Peloton Interactive, Inc. — Form 8-K
**Date of Report:** May 20, 2024

## Item 7.01 — Regulation FD Disclosure
In connection with the proposed global refinancing referred to in Item 8.01 below, Peloton
Interactive, Inc. has made available an investor presentation on its investor relations
website.

## Item 8.01 — Other Events

On May 20, 2024, the Company issued a press release to announce the launch of a **global
refinancing**, pursuant to which it is proposing to offer $275.0 million aggregate principal
amount of **convertible senior notes due 2029** (the "Notes") and enter into a **$1.0 billion
five-year term loan facility** and a **$100.0 million five-year revolving credit facility**
(together, the "New Credit Facilities"). The Notes will be sold to qualified institutional
buyers pursuant to Rule 144A under the Securities Act of 1933.

The Company intends to use the net proceeds of the Offering and the New Credit Facilities,
together with cash on hand, to **repurchase approximately $800.0 million aggregate principal
amount of its 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2026** (the "Existing Notes"), to refinance
its existing term loan and revolving credit facilities, and to pay related fees and expenses.
The entry into the New Credit Facilities is conditioned upon the repurchase of at least $800.0
million aggregate principal amount of the Existing Notes.
